text: - **HTTP** (`src/cli/mcp/http.rs`) — this is the one ChatGPT actually uses (the code even has a comment "ChatGPT requires HTTPS; tunnel this endpoint," line 65). Each POST is currently handled statelessly with **no session correlation at all** — no `Mcp-Session-Id` header is issued or read anywhere in `http.rs`. Per the MCP spec, `clientInfo` only arrives once, on `initialize`; without a session id to key a lookup by, a later `tools/call` from the same ChatGPT connection has no way to recover which `initialize` it belongs to. Threading `clientInfo.name` through for HTTP means first adding `Mcp-Session-Id` issuance/tracking (a small in-memory map + response header), which is a materially bigger change than "thread a field through." [...] - **`src/cli/mcp/http.rs`**: added `Mcp-Session-Id` tracking. On `initialize`, mints a session id (`mcp-<nanos>-<counter>`, matching the existing `mint_session_id()` convention), captures `clientInfo.name` from the request, stores it in a new `HttpState.mcp_sessions` map, and returns the id via the `Mcp-Session-Id` response header (also exposed/allowed via CORS). On every later request, reads the incoming `Mcp-Session-Id` header and looks up the stored name.
